# Comprehensive Geriatric Assessment CDS (smart-geri-cds) — Full Description

## Overview
An open-source Comprehensive Geriatric Assessment (CGA) clinical decision support tool that runs
entirely in the browser via the SMART on FHIR standard, with zero backend infrastructure.
Deployed as a static site (PWA, offline-capable) on GitHub Pages.

Target users: clinicians, care staff, and the general public assessing older adults' frailty and
functional status. Stratification axis: Clinical Frailty Scale (CFS) levels 1–9 (replacing any age band).

## URL
https://smart-geri-cds.yao.care/

## Source
https://github.com/yao-care/smart-geri-cds

---

## Functional Areas

### 1. Two assessment paths
- Public self-check (`/self-check/`): plain-language questionnaires, zh-TW text-to-speech, an
  in-memory (non-persisted) flow, and a green/amber/red summary with self-harm red-flag safety prompts.
- Professional assessment (`/assess/` → `/result/` → clinical workspace `/workspace/`): an entry gate
  sets the CFS level, then a three-tier pyramid asks a triage screen first and only expands to fuller
  scales for flagged domains.

### 2. CGA scoring and triage
- One YAML file per scale (`src/data/scales/`), scored against validated per-scale cut-offs.
- Severity model: normal / monitor / refer / incomplete (overall = worst across domains).
- Domains follow a two-level BGS Comprehensive Geriatric Assessment structure (physical,
  psychological, functional, social, environmental, future wishes).
- Results render a per-domain bar chart plus matched education and recommended videos.

### 3. SMART on FHIR upload (two routes)
- Connected hospital (fhirclient.js, in-page POST) — Standalone or EHR launch, OAuth2 PKCE.
- Redirect-type intake institutions such as GCM (`src/lib/fhir/intake-institutions.ts`): native
  fetch + Web Crypto PKCE with dynamic client registration; results upload as a FHIR transaction
  Bundle of Observation + DiagnosticReport (+ optional QuestionnaireResponse).
- Both share a single `/launch/` OAuth callback page.

### 4. Education content system
- Markdown article collection via Astro Content Collections (`src/data/education/`).
- A single source of truth, `content-relevance.yaml`, maps articles/videos to domain × CFS ×
  severity cells and drives three views: the `/education/` matrix, post-assessment recommendations,
  and trigger-based video lists.
- Video metadata lives in `src/data/video-catalog/*.yaml`. Pagefind full-text search (zh-TW).
- RSS feed at /rss.xml.

### 5. PDF report generation
- One-click client-side export via jsPDF (dynamic import) with Chinese font support.
- Reports reference the FHIR Patient ID only; no PII beyond what the report needs.

---

## Data Model

### Stratification — Clinical Frailty Scale (CFS 1–9)
The entry gate records a CFS level; scales declare which CFS levels they apply to.

### Severity (4 values)
- normal: within expected range
- monitor: follow up / observe
- refer: abnormal; further professional assessment or referral suggested
- incomplete: not enough completed to triage

---

## Architecture

- Build tool: Astro 5 (SSG, static output)
- UI components: Svelte 5 (runes, islands hydration)
- Charting: D3 submodules (d3-scale, d3-shape, …)
- Client storage: Dexie.js 4 (IndexedDB wrapper)
- FHIR client: fhirclient 2.x + native PKCE for redirect-type intake servers
- Search: Pagefind (WASM, in-browser)
- PDF: jsPDF (dynamic import)
- PWA: self-built service worker + manifest
- Deployment: GitHub Actions → GitHub Pages
- CI: pnpm + astro check + eslint + vitest + Lighthouse

## Content Structure

src/data/education/        — Markdown education articles + content-relevance.yaml (the single relevance source)
src/data/scales/           — CGA assessment scales (YAML, one per scale)
src/data/self-check/        — Public self-check question banks (YAML)
src/data/video-catalog/    — Education video metadata (YAML)
src/pages/                  — Routes (/assess /result /self-check /workspace /education /launch …)
public/llms.txt            — Brief LLM discovery file
public/llms-full.txt       — This file; comprehensive system description
pagefind.yml               — Pagefind search index configuration
